items.original_title: RuntimeError: source object number out of range
items.original_body:
## Describe the bug (mandatory)
When trying to combine two pdfs; I receive the following exception: `RuntimeError: source object number out of range`
```
doc.insertPDF(existing_doc)
 File "/dev-test-scripts/pdfenv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/fitz/fitz.py", line 4093, in insertPDF
 val = _fitz.Document_insertPDF(self, docsrc, from_page, to_page, start_at, rotate, links, annots, show_progress, final, _gmap)
RuntimeError: source object number out of range
```

## To Reproduce (mandatory)
I simply load two pdfs and try combine them. It works for 99% of pdfs but for a few i hit this issue and trying figure if there is a way to detect that there will be an issue before trying to merge.
```
o_doc = open(other_pdf_name, "rb").read()
u_doc = open(existing_pdf_name, "rb").read()

other_doc = fitz.Document('pdf', o_doc)
existing_doc = fitz.Document('pdf', u_doc)

other_doc.insertPDF(existing_doc)
```
I tried loading the PDFs directly using `fitz.Document(<filename>)`, but get the same results. I could even send you the PDF.

## Expected behavior (optional)
I expect the method to be successful.

## Your configuration (mandatory)
- MacOS, Catalina 10.15.7
- Python2.7 (ill try it on Python3 version to confirm)
- Whl from releases page. 1.18.3

Let me know if you want me to send the PDF that is breaking. Perhaps there is a method i could use to determine that the PDF is incompatible.

Let me know if you want me to send the PDF that is breaking. Perhaps there is a method i could use to determine that the PDF is incompatible.

items.original_title: joinCounts: Remove rejected variants from WT count
items.original_body: Use additional header elements to adjust effective depth\r\n\r\n#Rejected = Final - nomut - accepted = 1626810-(1365037+25790) = 235983
items.mutated_title: joinCounts: Remove rejected variants in WT count
items.mutated_body: Use additional header elements to adjust effective depth\r\n\r\n#Rejected = Final - nomut - accepted = 1626810-(1365037+25790) = 235983

items.original_title: New applications are not getting listed
items.original_body:
Running Cockpit using 238.1 tag
OS: Ubuntu 21.04
Page: Applications

Steps:
1. Installed Apache NiFi on my system.
2. In source code, created a directory named nifi under pkg.
3. Added two files under this directory. 
One is the normal **index.html** and another with the name **org.cockpit-project.cockpit-nifi.metainfo.xml** with the content as shown in attached image.

 **org.cockpit-project.cockpit-nifi.metainfo.xml**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/39826327/117310069-9bfd4800-aea0-11eb-8f9d-e0a121eff86a.png)

4. Added link to above xml file in Makefile.am
**Makefile.am**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/39826327/117309912-81c36a00-aea0-11eb-9a11-35ebcb67c72a.png)

items.original_title: ensure the config.js code gets the right id
items.original_body: Currently it only checks if the ID has length of 35 characters. Other properties within the file may have another ID with exactly the same length and chances is the regexp match will fetch the last occurrence.\r\n\r\nIt may be necessary to pull a fixed reference to the beginning of the file.
items.mutated_title: ensure the config.js code gets the right id
items.mutated_body: Currently it only checks if the ID has length of 35 characters. Other properties within the file may have another ID with exactly the same length and chances is the regexp match will fetch the last occurrence. It may be necessary to retrieve a fixed reference to the beginning of the file.

items.original_title: ALC Baseline Modelling Interface StartX/EndX Bug
items.original_body:
**Original reporter:** Stephen Cottrell / ISIS

### Expected behavior
You should not be able to move startx/endx beyond first and last good data limits, which is the current case for the general fitting window in workbench

### Actual behavior
You can move the limits beyond first and last which doesn't make sense for the fit and can result it not being able to drag them anymore without manually resetting them

### Steps to reproduce the behavior
1. Open ALC
2. Load some data (HIFI71480-500)*
3. Change log to field_main
4. Change max time to 12
5. Load data
6. Go to baseline modelling interface
7. Add polynomial fit function n=2
8. Add a section
9. Drag startx and endx beyond the first and last data values
10. This should not be possible
11. Click fit
12. You might now have one of the markers not be able to be dragged anymore

*Reduced amount of data used in beta test

### Platforms affected
all
